summary: Postinstall installs persistent C2 agent using npm registry as covert command/exfil channel
details: The package's postinstall script (install.js, referenced from package.json "postinstall": "node install.js") writes a copy of itself to /tmp/.sysm-agent.js and spawns a detached Node process that runs indefinitely on the installer's machine. The agent polls dist-tags for the npm package 'node-sysmetrics' on registry.npmjs.org for tags named 'cmdid' / 'cmd', base64-decodes the tag value, and executes it via `bash -c`. Command output is JSON- and base64-encoded, embedded in a synthesized package.json description field, and pushed with `npm publish --access public` under a Date.now-derived version, turning the npm registry into a covert exfiltration channel. install.js also writes a hardcoded npm auth token (assembled from split fragments with an inline comment stating the split is to evade static scanners) into the installer's ~/.npmrc via `npm config set //registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken...`, so the publishes run under the attacker's registry identity from the installer's host. The combination gives a remote operator arbitrary shell execution, persistence, and data exfiltration on any machine that installs the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
